notes: DGP denotes the data-generating process; n is sample size, p is dimensionality, pi is instrument strength, and tau is quantile.
Historical DML diagnostics absent from the 15-column schema are N/A, not zero. Use landscape orientation because the table is wide.
All differences use estimator A - estimator B; positive values mean estimator A has the larger metric.
Estimator groups follow Oracle, Post-selection, and DML order.
Instrument strength is ordered numerically, not lexicographically.
Unavailable DML warning and resolution diagnostics remain N/A. Use landscape orientation because the table is wide.
Associations are descriptive, not causal; unavailable DML warning and resolution diagnostics are N/A.
